posts.title: 18S tutorial
posts.excerpt: PhyloSuite: from data aquisition to phylogenetic tree annotation (single gene tree building)
posts.text: PhyloSuite: from data aquisition to phylogenetic tree annotation (single gene tree building) In order to make it easier for users to get started, we post a tutorial to briefly introduce the use of PhyloSuite. Here, we take ciliate as an example to demonstrate how to reconstruct phylogenetic tree based on 51 18S sequences (https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/j.1550-7408.2009.00413.x). 1. Sequence download and preparationThe accession number is as follows:1234567891011AF164122 X65150 AY547546 U17356 EU286810AF100301 U47620 M97909 L31520 EU039903Z29442 U97110 EU039887 Z29438 AY007AF060454 AY007445 U82204 X71134 X65149U57767 L26447 AF300281 AF530529 U17355AY331802 AY242119 AY331790 EU286812 DQ232761X71140 EU286811 AY331794 EU264561 EU286809X65151 U24248 AM292311 EU039899 DQ057347AF300286 EF123708 EU039896 L26446 U57765Z22931 AY007450 EU264560 AY378112 DQ834370U97112 First, download the above ID to the workspace of PhyloSuite (step 1, 2 below… corresponding to the figure, the same below) Select any of the working folders (here I choose files); Click Open file(s)/Input ID(s) to open the input window. Copy the above ID into the text box (separator spaces, line breaks, tabs, etc. are suppoted); Enter the mailbox (tell NCBI who is downloading the sequence); Click Start to download. After the sequence is downloaded to the workspace, we will find that some taxonomic groups are N/A, indicating that the taxonomic group is not recognized. We can select all the sequences, then right click, Get taxonomy, there will be a prompt after the selection, meaning To replace the current taxonomy, choose ok to start grabbing the taxonomic information from NCBI’s taxonomy database and replace it (Note, This is related to the subsequent phylogenetic tree annotation). After the above steps are completed, there may still be some IDs that are not recognized, indicating their taxonomic group is not in the latest classification system of NCBI. Or you will want to change the current lineage names. In these case, you can double-click the corresponding cell to enter the correct taxonomic group name. 2. Sequence extractionCtrl + A to select all the sequences, then right click and select Extract for sequence extraction. Right-click and choose Extract Since we are extracting 18S sequences, so we select 18S here; Select name to show in the extracted files, there are a number of informations, such as organism, ID, isolate, etc.. Since the article only shows the information of the class, you can select the class here, or you can also select the family; Here is to select the colors for marking taxonomic group on the phylogenetic tree in iTOL. You can double-click to modify the color. If the number of colors you set is less than the number of studied taxonomic groups, the rest will use random colors. For example, there are 11 classes, and I only set 5 colors, the rest 6 classes will use random colors; Click Start to run the program. After the extraction, enter the extract_results/rRNA folder (because 18S belongs to rRNA feature, so the folder name is rRNA). We found that 18S has many names, and the sequences with different names are in different files, so we need to unify the names first. Open the extract_results/StatFiles folder, find the name_for_unification.csv file, change the name to 18S in the New Name column, and save (you must save as csv format) Then go back to the window to extract the sequence Click the Settings button next to the sequence type to enter the settings interface; Drag the modified name_for_unification.csv file into the area indicated in the figure, and the modified name will be automatically recognized. Closing the settings window will automatically save the settings. At this point, you only need to run anew to get the 18S sequence after replacing the name. However, by opening the 18S sequence we will find only 42 sequences, and we have a total of 51 IDs. The reason here is because there are 3 sequences that are annotated as 16S-like genes (in fact, if you modify them to 18S in the previous table, you can also extract them. If you are interested, you can try them), the annotations of the 3 sequences are not recogniting (can be viewed from StatFiles/feature_unrecognized.csv), and 3 sequences did not find rRNA feature (can be viewed from StatFiles/ID_with_no_features.csv). These sequences that have not been successfully extracted require further operations. However, if you are confident that all the sequences are 18S, here is an easy way: Check default, and enter the output file name here; Start to extract You will see all 51 sequences was extracted and they are deposited in a file named “18S.fas” 3. Multiple sequence alignmentAfter the sequence is extracted, next we can go to the process of phylogenetic analysis. First, import the sequences we extracted into MAFFT for aligning. There are many ways to do this. Here are two more convenient ones: First, select the result folder extracted in the previous step, and then open MAFFT through Alignment-->MAFFT in the menu bar, the extracted sequence will be automatically imported into MAFFT; The second case is selecting MAFFT from the menu, then a pop-up window will automatically prompt that the input file available for the entire workspace has been prepared for MAFFT, and the result we just extracted is selected. Not just MAFFT, other functions can use these import files. Next is the sequence alignment First we need to delete the two 16S sequences (one has been deleted in the figure), because the extracted RNAs including 16S (noted above), which can be deleted by the delete button as shown in the figure (the button next to it can be used to view the sequence); Align the sequence with Normal mode; Other parameters can be set according to your own requirements. After settings, you can start. After the alignment is completed, you can view the sequence and modify it. Select the MAFFT result folder; Double-click the alignment result to open the sequence viewer to view the aligned sequence. If necessary, manual correction of the sequence can be performed in the same way as MEGA. 4. Nucleotide replacement model selectionNext is the model selection of the nucleotide sequence. Since ModelFinder does not recognize the results of MAFFT automatically (there is a way to use the Concatenation concatenation function as a bridge, as we will see later), so we need to manually import it. The operation is also simple: Double-click mafft_results to open the folder; Open the ModelFinder software window via Phylogeny-->ModelFinder in the menu bar and drag the aligned 18S sequence to the file input box of ModelFinder. Select Mrbayes in Model for to select the optimal models for Mrbayes; Other parameters can be selected according to your needs. After settings, you can click Start to start the program. 5. Phylogenetic tree reconstruction based on Bayesian method (BI) Double-click *.iqtree in the ModelFinder_results folder to view the selection result of the optimal model; click the ModelFinder_results folder; Open the Bayesian software Phylogeny-->MrBayes from the menu bar; At this point we can see that the sequence and model selection results have been automatically imported into the Mrbayes, and then one or more outgroups can be selected through the Outgroup drop-down menu; Select the generations we need to run; Other parameters can be selected according to your needs. After setting, you can click Start to start the program. 6. Phylogenetic tree reconstruction based on Maximum likelihood method (ML)Since IQ-TREE is able to select the best-fit model and then followed by phylogenetic tree reconstructing, you can completed them in this software in one step. Drag the aligned sequences directly into the IQ-TREE file input box. The model selects Auto, that is, selects the optimal model first, and then builds the tree according to the selected optimal model; You can choose +R to calculate the FreeRate model, which is recommended by the IQ-TREE author; Since 18S is small, it is best to use the standard bootstrap method based on the author’s recommendation; Other parameters can be selected according to your needs. After setting, you can click Start to start the program. 7. FlowchartIn addition to the above-mentioned conventional operation, PhyloSuite also provides a streamlined workflow mode, complete the steps from 3 to 6 by one-click. In this way, we only need to input the file in the first program, then it will run step-by-step (alignment–>concatenation–>model selection–>reconstruct the phylogenetic tree using Bayesian method/maximum likelihood methods), the results of the upstream program will be used as the input file of the downstream program, details as follows: Click Flowchart in the menu bar to open the program window. Select the analysis you want, here we choose MAFFT, Concatenation (although it is a single gene, the Concatenation must also be selected, because the downstream programs can only recognize concatenated results as input files, in this way, the Concatenation function served as format convertion), ModelFinder, IQ-TREE, and MrBayes; As mentioned above, we only need to input the file in the first program, so we click on the red Input file here to open the MAFFT program window; Click on the MAFFT file input box then the function that automatically prepare the input file mentioned above (see step 3) will pop up; Select the results we extracted before (note that only keep 18S); To save the input file or the parameters set in the MAFFT software, just close the window (also applied to other programs). After inputting the file into the MAFFT and setting the parameters, we may also need to set the parameters of other programs. Click the Check | Start button, a parameter summary window will pop up, allowing the user to modify and check the parameters; Click the blue words to set the parameters, the corresponding widgets will be highlighted; After setting the parameters for each software, just close the window to save the settings; PhyloSuite also provides the ability to automatically check the conflicting parameters. For example, if the model selected by Modelfinder does not use partition model, so MrBayes should also turn off partition mode. Click autocorrect to do this automatically. After the parameters are checked, you can click Ok, start to start the program. After running the program, you can view the result by the button next to the program name, and the programs IQ-TREE and MrBayes can be runned in parallel, because they are not upstream and downstream program relationships. After all the programs have finished, the main interface will have a summary description of the operation, as well as references to the software used, to facilitate articles written (note that for demo purpose, MrBayes and IQ-TREE have chosen the shortest running time.). 8. Phylogenetic Tree BeautificationAfter phylogenetic tree reconstruction, the last step is to annotate the phylogenetic tree. Here we take the results of IQ-TREE as an example. First open iTOL’s home page https://itol.embl.de/, log in to your account, and select My Trees; Double-click IQtree_results to open the tree of the IQ-TREE, and drag the *.treefile file into the area shown in the figure to automatically import the phylogenetic tree; Click on the tree to enter the editing interface. Double-click extract_results to open the results of we extracted before (step 2), and then enter the itolFiles folder. The files inside are used for iTOL annotation. The operation is very simple, just drag the file into the iTOL interface. As shown in the figure, after dragging in itol_gb_labels.txt, you can find that the name of the species has been replaced with the accession number (there is an error after dragging in, it is prompted that the listed ID is not found, because there are 9 species of sequences that have not been extracted, which will not affect our annotation so it can be ignored). In addition, itol_labels.txt and itol_ori_labels.txt are also capable of replacing names, you also can drag them to see the effect; Files such as itol_Class_ColourStrip.txt and itol_Class_Text.txt are used to mark the color of the class. a. Drag the itol_Class_ColourStrip.txt and itol_Class_Text.txt files together into the iTOL interface; b. Each file corresponds to a different datasets, you can click the jagged setting button in the figure, and set the parameters of each dataset; c. Set the Text size factor of Class text dataset to change the size of the name of the class; d. Set the Text size factor of color_strip dataset to change the width of the vertical block.   When we extracted the sequence (step 2), there is a position to set the color for the taxonomic groups? The colors of the five classes we set before can be found here, while the colors of other classes are randomly choosed. Note that the location of the taxonomic group name may not be in the middle, because it is randomly arranged, you can export to SVG format and fine-tune it with AI (Adobe Illustrator).  When there are hundreds or thousands of species, or when you want to compare multiple topologies based on the same dataset, the convenience of this annotation method is fully reflected, and according to the color of the annotation, Groups and multi-line groups are also easy to view. Similar to ML tree, when annotating BI tree, just drag the *.con.tre tree file into iTOL, and the rest of the operations is the same as the IQ-TREE tree. At this point, the single-gene phylogenetic analysis process is finished. This is a complete operation step from data aquisition to phylogenetic tree annotation. Of course, you can start from any step. For example, it is possible to start directly from the MAFFT multiple sequence alignment, but in this way there is no way to annotate the evolution tree. Software download addresshttps://github.com/dongzhang0725/PhyloSuite/releases ReferenceZhang, D., Gao, F., Li, WX, Jakovlić, I., Zou, H., Zhang, J., and Wang, GT (2018). PhyloSuite: an integrated and scalable desktop platform for streamlined molecular sequence data Management and evolutionary phylogenetics studies. bioRxiv, doi: 10.1101/489088.

posts.title: Hello World
posts.text: Welcome to Hexo! This is your very first post. Check documentation for more info. If you get any problems when using Hexo, you can find the answer in troubleshooting or you can ask me on GitHub. Quick StartCreate a new post1$ hexo new "My New Post" More info: Writing Run server1$ hexo server More info: Server Generate static files1$ hexo generate More info: Generating Deploy to remote sites1$ hexo deploy More info: Deployment
